examples: In domains where data is king, or where data privacy is king, it's not very comforting, or copacetic, to just send it off to the ether-like abstraction of Bigtable.
All was copacetic until the plane hit some severe unanticipated thunderstorms, with lots of lightning.
Some day all of us will be copacetic with electronically stored information and the lawsuit discovery process will be coextensive with eDiscovery.
While the rating agencies mutter about downgrading Treasury debt, the markets seem absolutely copacetic about the risks of a late summer U.S. bond default.
